New qspibus support for displayio. Stability improvements to USB SD card handling. Merge of MicroPython v1.27. Update to

trends.headline: Germany's Merz: Climate protection must not hold economy back
trends.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said the public would not accept climate policies that lead to deindustrialization, warning that they would hinder progress and innovation.

trends.headline: Former UCSF doctor claims university defamed him in 2022 report on unethical prison experiments
trends.summary: Dr. Howard Maibach says the university's statements that he conducted unethical prison medical experiments in the 1960s and 1970s are not true and highly defamatory.

trends.headline: Sam Altman’s Orb Company Promoted a Bruno Mars Partnership That Doesn't Exist
trends.summary: “To be clear, we were never approached … nor were we in any discussions regarding a partnership or tour access,” a spokesperson for the artist tells WIRED.

trends.headline: Crypto scam lures ships into Strait of Hormuz, falsely promising safe passage
trends.summary: Ship attacked by Iran after possibly falling for safe passage crypto scam.

trends.headline: US Senate Candidate Caught Insider Trading on Kalshi Says He Did It on Purpose
trends.summary: Mark Moran, an underdog Senate candidate from Virginia, claims he wanted to get caught violating the prediction market platform’s rules.

trends.headline: Lifeline or death knell? Colorado bill to speed abandonment of town with failing waterworks advances
trends.summary: The local government in the rural town of Hartman, Colorado, resigned in January, leaving no one to run elections or maintain the town’s water supply.

trends.headline: Scientists Gave Cocaine to Salmon and You Will Absolutely Believe What Happened Next
trends.summary: After scientists exposed wild fish to cocaine and a cocaine metabolite, they observed that, as in the lab, fish on cocaine do not act like normal fish.

trends.headline: New York falafel-taco fusion joint accuses Visa, Mastercard of price-fixing monopoly over card fees
trends.summary: Three small businesses say the companies' market power forces them to pay inflated fees every time a customer swipes a credit card.

trends.headline: Tesla reports Q1 2026 earnings: Still profitable
trends.summary: Car sales are up, battery sales and emissions credits are down.

trends.headline: Our newsroom AI policy
trends.summary: How Ars Technica uses, and doesn't use, generative AI.

trends.headline: Lawsuit: Nintendo is getting tariff refunds—its customers should get them instead
trends.summary: Lawsuit demands Nintendo pass Trump tariff refunds on to its customers.

trends.headline: Boards Of Canada announce ‘Inferno’ – their first album in 13 years
trends.summary: The Scottish duo's fifth studio album is set to drop on on May 29 via Warp and will feature 18 tracks

trends.headline: School's out in Iran: No phone, no internet, no classes
trends.summary: Iran's schools are closed because of the war. Classes are being held via TV programs and the national intranet. Children without a laptop or smartphone are left out. The country is effectively cut off from the internet.

trends.headline: Printed Neurons Communicating with Living Brain Cells
trends.summary: To move closer to a biological model, Mark Hersam’s team developed artificial neurons using soft, printable materials that better mimic the brain’s structure and behavior. The backbone of that advance is a series of electronic inks. Photo by Mark Hersam via Northwestern University Engineers at North

trends.headline: It’s Official: ‘Virgin River’ Season 8 is Filming (and Martin Henderson is Back Directing!)
trends.summary: The series is back up in Vancouver for its record breaking eight season!
